purpose: Fixes a Bluetooth HCI command queuing issue to prevent use-after-free when HCI_UNREGISTER is set.
Removes an unnecessary asm/termios.h include in n_hdlc.c since it's already included via linux/tty.h.
The commit updates documentation to include the 'N/A' metric for store operations in perf c2c reports.
Fixes vma lock handling issues during splitting and unmapping in hugetlb, addressing race conditions and ensuring proper lock allocation.
The commit documents AVDD and MICVDD supplies in the dt-bindings for the realtek,rt5682s codec.
Fixes resource leak by adding missing iounmap() in remove function.
The commit removes an unused work field from a struct in the mlx5e driver for kTLS.
The commit changes an unsigned integer in a struct to a signed one to prevent static checker warnings when assigning -1.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs for RISC-V without functional changes, improving configuration structure.
The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace open-coded checks for CAN-FD mode configuration in the chip's FIFOs.
Modifies self-tests for the RTC subsystem to skip execution when RTC hardware is absent, avoiding unnecessary test failures.
The commit removes an unnecessary variable assignment that was causing a compiler warning. It improves code quality by eliminating dead code.
The commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks to prevent unnecessary inclusion when CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y, reducing kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase 'be split' in spte.h, improving code clarity.
Reverts a previous change causing underflow by populating the dcn315 clk table based on dcfclk states.
Adds device tree binding documentation for the Mali-G31 GPU on the RZ/V2L SoC.
The commit renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for naming consistency in the DW PCIe core driver.
Fixes a clang -Wformat warning by correcting the format specifier to match promoted integer type.
Fixes a MIPS jump_label issue by casting upper bound to long for signed comparison, preventing negative offset warnings.
The commit removes a redundant case in SetHwReg8188EU() to call a function directly, part of eliminating the hal layer.
The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in the arm64 kernel code, improving code quality.
Removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes as the provider only uses a single cell.
The commit allocates arrays in struct ctrl_ref to prepare for dynamic resizing by drivers.
Adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs in the socinfo driver to recognize more hardware models.
The commit updates the rtl8xxxu Wi-Fi driver to recognize all possible chip versions (A-P) instead of only up to E, preventing 'unknown' messages for higher versions.
The commit removes redundant .is_enabled state from PRCMU clocks since the core already manages enable counts, simplifying the code.
The commit aims to refactor RISC-V mm initialization code by removing unnecessary #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL directives, grouping similar usages together for better maintainability.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree for sm6350-lena, preparing for I2C node additions.
The commit improves comment clarity by renaming variables, fixing typos, and adjusting comment formatting for style compliance.
Fixes an infinite loop in release_swfw_sync when HW semaphore acquisition fails due to hardware removal.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology for clocking, replacing old-style defines.
The commit removes redundant code related to IS_SWAPFILE in NFS, as it is always defined.
Implements GTP echo response handling to comply with specifications, adding necessary functionality for Echo Request and Response messages.

The commit replaces certain cpumask functions (cpumask_next_*) with cpumask_first_* where appropriate and introduces a new function cpumask_first_zero(). This appears to be an optimization or refactoring effort.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver because the firmware no longer supports it. This prevents an error message from appearing after roaming.
Adds support for Intel platforms using alc5682 without an amplifier by introducing a new board config and connecting to SSP0.
The commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg requests fail when unsupported by the socket, preventing incorrect behavior in such cases.
The commit modifies LaTeX document generation to check for xeCJK.sty before loading, preventing errors on systems where the package is missing or broken.
The commit modifies the checkpatch script to stop flagging certain 'case' macros as errors. It addresses existing usage in kernel drivers and improves code analysis accuracy.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers to improve code architecture and sharing among drivers. No functional changes are made.
Adds a JPEG decode worker interface to enable parallel execution of three HW units on MT8195, improving media processing.
The commit removes redundant VPRINTK() calls and a helper function in libata, as their information is already covered by tracepoints.
Adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank in FREESYNC mode to improve power efficiency.
Fixes type mismatch causing CFI failure by updating function prototype to match actual implementation.
The commit corrects the 'resets' property name in device tree bindings for STM32 pinctrl drivers and adds validation to prevent future errors.
The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 for the ath9k wireless driver. It silences the warning, suggesting either a potential bug fix or suppression of a false positive.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the crypto driver for sun8i-ss to simplify code.

The commit reorganizes file installation and removal functions within the io_uring subsystem by moving them to the filetable code. It also modifies helper functions to use a context instead of an io_kiocb, improving code structure.
The commit updates device tree bindings to separate G1 and G2 decoder blocks under vpu-blk-ctrl for independent handling.
The commit removes an unused macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON) and consolidates its content into another macro, simplifying the configuration.
